revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:1 nguŏk 16 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:1月16日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 1월 16일]][[zh-min-nan:1 goe̍h 16 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:正月十六]][[zh:正月十六]]
{{Not to be confused with|January 16|qq=January 16}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=一月十六|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 1월 16일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar January|qq=}} '''January 16''' is the 16th day of the month {{ill|Zhengyue|en|Zhengyue|qid=Q1072983|lt=January (Mutsuki)|1=Zhengyue}}, the 1st month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}}, and the day is designated as {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu|1=Butsumetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10886789}}.
== Events == * In the 6th year of the Tenshō Shōhō era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|754|en|754|qid=Q23646|lt=754|1=754}} {{ill|February 12|en|February 12|qid=Q2336|lt=February 12|1=February 12}}) – the eminent Chinese monk {{ill|Jianzhen|en|Jianzhen|qid=Q714061|lt=Ganjin|1=Jianzhen}} accompanied the deputy envoy to Tang, {{ill|Ōtomo Komamaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Komamaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11432332}}, on his voyage to Japan. * In the 1st year of the Ansei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1854|en|1854|qid=Q7670|lt=1854|1=1854}} {{ill|February 13|en|February 13|qid=Q2333|lt=February 13|1=February 13}}) – Commander of the U.S. East India Fleet, Admiral {{ill|Perry (disambiguation)|en|Perry (disambiguation)|qid=Q224607|lt=Matthew Perry|1=Perry (disambiguation)}}, arrived in Japan once again.
== Births == * In the 5th year of the Kōwa era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1103|en|1103|qid=Q12487|lt=1103|1=1103}} {{ill|February 24|en|February 24|qid=Q2355|lt=February 24|1=February 24}}) – Emperor Toba, the 74th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1156|en|1156|qid=Q19653|lt=1156|1=1156}}). * In the 6th year of the Kempo era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1218|en|1218|qid=Q5381|lt=1218|1=1218}} {{ill|February 12|en|February 12|qid=Q2336|lt=February 12|1=February 12}}) – Fujiwara Yoritoki, the 4th Shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (died {{ill|1256|en|1256|qid=Q5502|lt=1256|1=1256}}).
== Deaths == * In the 87th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|399|en|399|qid=Q48150|lt=399|1=399}} {{ill|February 7|en|February 7|qid=Q2322|lt=February 7|1=February 7}}) – Emperor Nintoku, the 16th Emperor of Japan (born {{ill|257|en|257|qid=Q33737|lt=257|1=257}}). * In the 5th year of Emperor Seining’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|484|en|484|qid=Q30981|lt=484|1=484}} {{ill|February 27|en|February 27|qid=Q2361|lt=February 27|1=February 27}}) – Emperor Seining, the 22nd Emperor of Japan (born {{ill|444|en|444|qid=Q33433|lt=444|1=444}}?). * In the 10th year of the Tenshō era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1582|en|1582|qid=Q6719|lt=1582|1=1582}} {{ill|February 18|en|February 18|qid=Q2343|lt=February 18|1=February 18}}) – Sakuhama Nobumori, a Sengoku warlord and retainer of the Oda family (born {{ill|1528|en|1528|qid=Q6415|lt=1528|1=1528}}). * In the 2nd year of the Ansei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1855|en|1855|qid=Q7673|lt=1855|1=1855}} {{ill|March 4|en|March 4|qid=Q2396|lt=March 4|1=March 4}}) – Egawa Eiryu, an official of Izu Nirasayama and military scholar (born {{ill|1801|en|1801|qid=Q6877|lt=1801|1=1801}}).
== Commemorations == * Yabu-iri (Ritual of Entering the Thicket) * Enma Sai-nichi (Enma Competition Day) * Jūroku-nichi-sai (Sixteenth Day Festival)<ref>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-443254.html "New Year's in the Other World": A festival celebrating the spirits held by families, with a grand singing event] (Ryukyu Shimpo), January 12, 2017</ref>
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|1|15|1|17|12|16|2|1|0116|qq=}}
== References == {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
